title: CLI exit codes are coarse (0/1/2) vs the scripts' 1-5 diagnostic granularity
description: The orchestrator scripts return granular exit codes (1-5: usage / not-initialized / schema-absent / validation / etc.); the CLI returns only 0/1/2, so callers and CI cannot distinguish error classes.

evidence.reference: CLI maps to 0/1/2 only (UsageError + generic catch)
scripts use 1-5 exit-code granularity (e.g. dry-run FAIL exits 5)
GAP-8 in the parity survey, categorized CLI-only-shared-helper

impact: Scripted/CI consumers of the CLI cannot branch on error class (arg vs not-initialized vs schema-absent vs validation), below the scripts' diagnostic resolution.
proposed_resolution: Map error classes to codes (arg/usage -> 2, not-initialized/BootstrapNotFoundError -> 1, schema-absent -> 3, validation -> 5) at the cli.ts catch + UsageError sites (:456-462, :511-518). The op never sees it. Re-confirm via the canonical pipeline.
